All right, so let's dig into the 10x sales funnel flowcharts. We've got three different flowcharts we're going to show you here. We've got the appointment funnel flowchart, the product funnel flowchart, and the webinar funnel flowchart. And we're going to talk about which one you should use and which situations, but the important thing that you need to understand here is that these are just examples. So for example, you see here that there's three web pages and there's three emails that are going out. This by no means is how you have to set it up. And in fact, in many situations, you don't want to set it up like this. You're going to set it up in whatever way you think is best based on your 10x training. So let's just dig into the 10x appointment funnel here. This is one that's designed for companies that don't necessarily sell with their online funnels, but rather they generate appointments. So you're going to use this if you sell high ticket items or maybe some kind of membership for gyms or whatever it might be. So generally speaking, you're going to want to have some sort of landing page that's going to opt people in. And just to give you a prime example here, you don't even need to have a landing page before the appointment booking page, but a lot of times you're going to want to. It's kind of a small commitment leading to a big commitment. So with the landing page, you might, for example, just get first name, last name, email and phone number. Whereas on the appointment booking page, you might ask for more details such as what are your specific goals and et cetera, et cetera, et cetera. So in this particular funnel, someone lands on an opt-in page after opting in, they go to an appointment booking page. Generally speaking, that booking page will probably want to have some videos or some other things. And then we're setting them along to a content page. Maybe you want to have on there some success stories or testimonials or a little bit more about yourself so they can learn who you are and what you're all about. The important thing to remember is whenever you're setting up funnels, you're always going to have automated emails working with your pages. So when somebody opts in, you're going to send them a sequence which we refer to as an indoctrination sequence. Meaning, you're indoctrinating them. They're getting to know you. You're really starting to establish a relationship with them. So in this, you see that there's only a couple emails. It could be a 10 email sequence. It could be a one email sequence, whatever it is that you want. And then whenever somebody books an appointment, generally speaking, you're going to send them a reminder. So all of this is covered more in the other manuals, but this is a good basic flow in which you can use for an appointment funnel if you want to use that kind. But you also might be using, say for example, you sell books or products or any kind of widget or a membership, then you're going to use a product funnel. So this is a very basic classic example where again, the first page is a landing page, meaning you're grabbing an opt-in before you send them to the sales page. Now here's a very important note. As you may or may not want to use an opt-in page before the sales page, it really depends on what it is that you sell. So for example, if you sell a product that's $495, then it might be tough to make a sale on that initial visit from a visitor. So that's why you'd want to use a landing page so that way you can continue to communicate with the person and try to sell them the high ticket item. But if you sell the product for $7, you may find it a ways to use an opt-in page. So to continue down the flow here, after the sales page, let's say that they buy, well let's say they click on the buy button, you might have another page that's got an order form on it, and that order form could have what's called a bump offer. And again, we talk about this more in the manual and in the training, but a bump offer is when you add, yes, I want to add that and they check a box and they take it. So after the order form, they could go to a one click upsell page and then to an order confirmation page. Again, this is just a basic flow. There could be any number of variations of this. And then don't forget after an opt-in, you're going to have an indoctrination sequence and then after what you're going to want is when somebody comes in and they give you an opt-in, you're going to have a sales follow up that's going to continue to send them back to that sales page or maybe to a different sales page. It's important that you just understand the concepts and you realize that this is completely flexible and then if they buy, you're going to want to send them a buyer follow up, etc. Okay. So that's just a very basic example of a product funnel that you can turn into anything that you want. And now let's look at probably the most advanced type of funnel that we share here, which is a webinar funnel. What's unique about a webinar is you always need to start out with a webinar registration page. What's allows people to sign up, they choose a time, they choose a date, whatever it might be. This could be either a live webinar or it could be an automated webinar. We use both depending on what the situation is. And then after they register, they go to a webinar confirmation page, which is just going to give them the details. It might have an add to calendar link. It's just really going to get them excited and make sure that they attend the webinar. And then they're going to go when they attend to the webinar broadcast room. So they're either going to go there directly from the webinar confirmation page or from an event reminder email that's sent to them. But when they get on the webinar, assuming you're selling something, you're then going to send them to a sales page and then to an order page and then to an order confirmation page and you'll have sales follow up that happens all throughout this. So here's the important thing to understand here is that these are examples and rather than just teaching you concepts, what we're going to do is we're going to give you our best funnels in our swipe files so that you can see specific examples of exactly how we do this. And then you're going to notice, for example, when you go into our webinar swipe file is that we have a very different configuration than this. And in fact, on our webinar swipe file, we're going to show you very advanced functionality that will put people into different buckets and follow up depending on their activity, how much of the webinar they watch, et cetera, et cetera. So the important thing is again, remember, you build this how you want. Always start out basic and go from there. But as long as you understand the concept of these are building blocks, people go from one page to the next and we've always got automated follow up happening, then you're going to be just fine.
